rationale: The demonstrated LLM capability ceiling (basic web API vulnerability discovery, simple Python scripting, input validation identification) has limited but real relevance to internal network discovery — primarily in accelerating enumeration script generation and interpreting scan output rather than enabling qualitatively new techniques. With the OC3 actor already possessing strong operational capabilities and standard IT tools available post-privilege escalation, the LLM provides modest uplift of approximately 3-4 percentage points above the baseline mode. The primary failure modes at this step (SOC detection, network segmentation, deception technologies) are not meaningfully addressed by this capability level, justifying a conservative probability distribution centered around 88%.
